evidence: Accelerating artificial intelligence enablement and responsibly leveraging data, analytics, and digital capabilities to deliver smarter, faster outcomes across the businesses
continuing to invest in its underwriting systems and capabilities...expanding its use of data analytics, artificial intelligence capabilities and third party data
The Company seeks to drive greater efficiency, shorten the quoting process and improve the customer s experience through expanded use of digital and artificial intelligence capabilities
Through its industry-leading ICON quoting tool, The Hartford quotes over 75% of new business policies from admitted markets without human intervention.

Emerging artificial intelligence-enabled capabilities are raising customer and distributor expectations for speed and automation and may further reshape how small business customers shop for coverage.
expanding its use of data analytics, artificial intelligence capabilities and third party data to make expert risk selection and pricing decisions
The NAIC adopted a Model Bulletin on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems by Insurers. About 25 states have adopted some form of the Model Bulletin.

Use of artificial intelligence by us or by such third parties could also result in inadvertent disclosure of our confidential information...could result in legal liability, regulatory action and reputational harm.
Executive Artificial Intelligence Governance Council [listed as named oversight body under ERCC enterprise risk governance structure]

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts, GPU counts, or explicit AI-dedicated budget figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 (named initiatives, no quantified scale).

The ICON quoting tool statistic (75% of policies without human intervention) is the primary quantified operational metric; most other AI narrative is directional rather than outcome-quantified, limiting concreteness confidence.
Capex_mentions list is empty because no dollar or capacity figures tied to AI/compute investment appear in the excerpts.

Risk disclosure scored 5 based on named governance body (Executive AI Governance Council), named regulatory frameworks (NAIC Model Bulletin, ~25 states), and specific risk mechanisms (confidentiality breach, regulatory action, reputational harm); however, no quantified risk exposure figures are disclosed.
